columns.name: l_orderkey
columns.index_rationale: PK-FK join with orders table. Hash index provides O(1) lookup for join probing. Used in Q3 aggregation grouping.
columns.encoding_rationale: High cardinality foreign key, no obvious pattern for compression.

columns.name: l_linenumber
columns.index_rationale: Part of composite PK but not used in queries.
columns.encoding_rationale: Small range (1-7 typically), but not accessed. Skip encoding overhead.

columns.name: l_quantity
columns.index_rationale: Range predicate (l_quantity < 24) in Q6. Sequential scan with vectorized filter is faster than index for medium selectivity (~46%).
columns.encoding_rationale: Continuous values, no clear compression pattern. Delta encoding not beneficial for non-monotonic data.

columns.name: l_extendedprice
columns.index_rationale: Used only in arithmetic expressions for aggregation. No filter or join predicates.
columns.encoding_rationale: High cardinality continuous values. Floating point compression yields minimal benefit and adds decompression overhead in hot aggregation path.

columns.name: l_discount
columns.index_rationale: BETWEEN predicate in Q6. Sequential scan with SIMD filter is optimal for range predicates.
columns.encoding_rationale: Fixed precision decimal (0.00-0.10), but stored as double. Converting to int16_t (basis points) would enable better compression, but keep as-is for simplicity.

columns.name: l_tax
columns.encoding_rationale: Similar to l_discount, no compression benefit.

columns.name: l_returnflag
columns.index_rationale: Low-cardinality (3 values: R/A/N). Aggregation scan is sequential; no index needed. Hash aggregation handles grouping efficiently.
columns.encoding_rationale: Extremely low cardinality (3 values). Dictionary encoding: 8-byte string → 1-byte code. ~8x compression. Integer comparison in aggregation is 10x faster than string comparison.

columns.name: l_linestatus
columns.index_rationale: Low-cardinality (2 values: O/F). Sequential aggregation scan.
columns.encoding_rationale: Extremely low cardinality (2 values). Dictionary encoding: 8-byte string → 1-byte code. ~8x compression, faster comparisons.

columns.name: l_shipdate
columns.index_rationale: CRITICAL: Used in range predicates in all 3 queries (Q1: <=, Q6: >= and <, Q3: >). Sorted index enables zone maps for block-level data skipping. With 7-year range (1992-1998) and selective filters (14% in Q6), zone maps can skip 80-90% of blocks. Binary search on sorted data gives O(log n) range bound lookup.
columns.encoding_rationale: DATE stored as days_since_epoch. Monotonic increasing in sorted order. Delta encoding: 32-bit dates → 8-bit deltas (daily increments). 4x compression. Zone maps provide min/max per block for pruning.

columns.name: l_commitdate
columns.encoding_rationale: Even though not accessed, dates benefit from delta encoding. Minimal overhead, 4x compression if scanned.

columns.name: l_shipinstruct
columns.encoding_rationale: TPC-H spec defines 4 shipping instructions. Very low cardinality. Dictionary encoding: 25-byte string → 1-byte code. 25x compression.

columns.name: l_shipmode
columns.encoding_rationale: TPC-H spec defines 7 ship modes. Low cardinality. Dictionary encoding: 10-byte string → 1-byte code. 10x compression.

columns.name: l_comment
columns.encoding_rationale: Free-text comments, high cardinality. Not accessed in queries. No compression to avoid overhead.

lineitem.storage_notes: CRITICAL TABLE: Columnar storage mandatory. Physically sort by l_shipdate to maximize zone map effectiveness (all queries filter on date). Column layout optimization: Store hot columns (l_shipdate, l_orderkey, l_extendedprice, l_discount, l_quantity, l_returnflag, l_linestatus, l_tax) contiguously for cache efficiency. Zone maps: 8192-row blocks with min/max on l_shipdate, l_discount, l_quantity for Q6 predicate pushdown. Late materialization: Filter on l_shipdate first (produces selection vector), then materialize other columns only for qualifying rows.

columns.name: o_orderkey
columns.index_rationale: Primary key. Hash index for O(1) join with lineitem (FK lookup). Also used in Q3 grouping.
columns.encoding_rationale: Primary key, high cardinality, random distribution.

columns.name: o_custkey
columns.index_rationale: Foreign key to customer. Hash index for join probing. Q3 filters customer first (20% selectivity), then probes orders by o_custkey. Hash lookup is critical for join performance.

columns.name: o_orderstatus
columns.encoding_rationale: Low cardinality (3 values: O/F/P). Dictionary encoding: 1-byte char → even smaller with bit packing, but minimal gain. Use dictionary for consistency.

columns.name: o_orderdate
columns.index_rationale: Range filter in Q3 (o_orderdate < '1995-03-15'). Sorted index enables zone maps for ~50% data pruning. Also used in Q3 grouping/sorting, but sorting happens after aggregation (small result set).
columns.encoding_rationale: DATE as days_since_epoch. Delta encoding for 4x compression. Zone maps for range pruning.

columns.name: o_orderpriority
columns.encoding_rationale: Low cardinality (5 priorities in TPC-H). Dictionary encoding: 15-byte string → 1-byte code.

columns.name: o_clerk
columns.encoding_rationale: High cardinality (1000 clerks per SF). No compression benefit.

columns.name: o_shippriority
columns.index_rationale: Used in Q3 grouping/ordering, but after aggregation (small result set). No index needed.
columns.encoding_rationale: Single value (0) in standard TPC-H. RLE would compress to near-zero, but not worth the complexity.

orders.storage_notes: Columnar storage. Physically sort by o_orderdate for zone map effectiveness in Q3. Hash index on o_orderkey and o_custkey pre-built for joins. Zone maps on o_orderdate with 8192-row blocks enable 50% data skipping in Q3.

columns.name: c_custkey
columns.index_rationale: Primary key. Hash index for join with orders. Q3 filters customer first, then uses PK for join build phase.

columns.name: c_nationkey
columns.encoding_rationale: Low cardinality (25 nations), but not accessed. Skip encoding.

columns.name: c_mktsegment
columns.index_rationale: Equality filter (c_mktsegment = 'BUILDING') in Q3 with 20% selectivity. Small table (150K rows), sequential scan with dictionary-coded comparison is faster than index. Vectorized scan with SIMD string comparison or dictionary code lookup is optimal.
columns.encoding_rationale: CRITICAL: Low cardinality (5 segments). Dictionary encoding enables integer comparison in filter (10x faster than string comparison). 10-byte string → 1-byte code.

customer.storage_notes: Columnar storage. Small dimension table. Pre-filter by c_mktsegment (dictionary-encoded) before join. Hash index on c_custkey for join build phase. Consider clustering by c_mktsegment for better cache locality during scan, but not critical given small size.

columns.name: n_nationkey
columns.index_rationale: Primary key. Tiny table (25 rows), but hash index is trivial and enables consistent join interface.

nation.storage_notes: Tiny dimension table. Row storage acceptable, but use columnar for consistency. Entire table fits in L1 cache (few KB). No special optimizations needed.

region.storage_notes: Tiny dimension table. Fits in L1 cache. No optimizations needed.

columns.name: s_suppkey
columns.index_rationale: Primary key. Not used in queries, but hash index is standard for PKs.

supplier.storage_notes: Not accessed in sample queries. Basic columnar storage, no special optimizations.

columns.name: p_mfgr
columns.encoding_rationale: Not accessed, but low cardinality (5 manufacturers). Could use dictionary if accessed.

columns.name: p_brand
columns.encoding_rationale: Not accessed, but low cardinality (25 brands).

part.storage_notes: Not accessed in sample queries. Basic columnar storage.

partsupp.storage_notes: Not accessed in sample queries. Basic columnar storage.

design_rationale: This design optimizes for the scan-heavy OLAP workload with emphasis on lineitem (hot table). Key decisions: (1) Columnar storage for all tables enables projection pushdown and SIMD vectorization. (2) Sorted indexes with zone maps on date columns (l_shipdate, o_orderdate) provide 50-90% data skipping for range predicates. Physical sorting by date creates clustered storage for maximum zone map effectiveness. (3) Hash indexes on all PK-FK join keys (o_orderkey, c_custkey, o_custkey) enable O(1) hash joins. (4) Dictionary encoding for low-cardinality strings (flags, segments, ship modes) reduces memory by 8-25x and enables integer comparisons. (5) Delta encoding for dates exploits monotonicity for 4x compression. (6) No indexes on filter columns with medium selectivity (l_discount, l_quantity) - sequential SIMD scans are faster than index overhead. (7) Late materialization strategy: filter on indexed/encoded columns first, materialize other columns only for qualifying rows. Trade-offs: Sorted indexes increase build time but pay off with zone map pruning. Dictionary encoding adds encode/decode overhead but dominates for low-cardinality (<100 unique values). Hash indexes use 20-50% extra memory but are mandatory for hash joins.
summary: Columnar storage throughout. Critical optimizations: (1) lineitem physically sorted by l_shipdate with zone maps for 80-90% block pruning, (2) dictionary encoding on all low-cardinality strings (8-25x compression, 10x faster comparisons), (3) hash indexes on all join keys (o_orderkey, c_custkey, o_custkey) for O(1) lookups, (4) delta encoding on date columns for 4x compression. Expected performance: Q1/Q6 benefit from columnar scan + SIMD + late materialization (5-10x speedup). Q3 benefits from customer filter (20% selectivity) + zone map pruning on orders/lineitem dates (50% each) + hash joins (10x faster than nested loop). Overall: 10-20x speedup vs naive row storage with full scans.
